advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lifespan test where start_background_janitor raises, asserting the context still yields (receiver serves) and shutdown completes without error (janitor_stop stays None).
evidence: src/rebar/review_bot/app.py:83-88: `try: from rebar._snapshot import start_background_janitor ... except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a janitor failure must not take down the receiver / logger.exception(...)`
src/rebar/review_bot/app.py:92-93: `if janitor_stop is not None: janitor_stop.set()` — the None-guard exists ONLY for the startup-failure path and is exercised by no test
The only new test (`fake_start` at tests/unit/test_review_bot.py:1090) always returns cleanly; search for 'janitor failed' finds only the source log line, confirming no test drives the except branch

Regression blindness: removing the try/except or the `is not None` guard would leave every test green while breaking the 'gate matters more than the janitor' guarantee (shutdown would AttributeError when startup failed)
advisory.finding: The janitor's non-fatal-failure path — the core design contract of this diff ('a janitor failure must not take down the receiver') — is untested. No test makes start_background_janitor raise to verify the exception is swallowed, the lifespan still yields (receiver serves), and shutdown does not blow up while janitor_stop is None.
scenarios: start_background_janitor raises at startup (e.g. disk/env error building JanitorConfig) — receiver must still serve and shut down cleanly
advisory.suggested_fix: Add a sibling test that monkeypatches start_background_janitor to raise, then `async with appmod.lifespan(appmod.app): ...` and assert the body runs and the context exits cleanly (no exception on shutdown).

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the janitor's non-fatal startup-failure path.
coaching.subject: the janitor's non-fatal startup-failure path
